title: Repo foundation
description: Establish the initial Roger Reviewer monorepo structure, operating docs, release and install path, and architecture-spike track so later implementation is grounded in a coherent local-first workspace rather than ad hoc package creation.
acceptance_criteria: Workspace, docs, release or install path, and packaging prerequisites are explicit; core architecture spikes and ADR outcomes are captured as child tasks; extension packaging does not become a hidden blocker for the local product

title: Add an initial README and curate the first GitHub-facing planning snapshot
description: Create a contributor-facing top-level README for Roger Reviewer, clean up ignore rules for transient local artifacts, and define a lean first commit that captures the planning/docs core without vendoring heavyweight local exploration clones.

title: Define release artifact matrix and CI/CD ownership
description: Specify the supported Roger 0.1.0 artifact classes, target platforms, checksum or signing expectations, and which CI or release jobs own build, packaging, and publication.
acceptance_criteria: macOS, Windows, and Linux artifact classes are explicit; build, checksum or signing, packaging, and publication ownership is assigned; browser-extension artifacts and Native Messaging assets are defined as separate optional release work

title: Define one-line install and update flow for the local product
description: Specify the artifact-driven install and update path for the Roger CLI and local companion surface so local-first usage stays real even when the extension is absent.
acceptance_criteria: One-line install and update flows are explicit; host-platform detection, version selection, and checksum behavior are defined; extension installation stays optional and separate from the local product path

title: Domain and storage
description: Define the durable Roger data model for sessions, runs, findings, artifacts, approval-safe outbound communication, scope/memory overlays, and rebuildable search sidecars before UI work spreads. This epic must lock the canonical local-first store shape, write-ownership rules, and migration boundaries so later CLI, TUI, and extension work do not invent conflicting state models or daemon-like coordination assumptions.
acceptance_criteria:
Core entities, invariants, and aggregate boundaries are explicit for sessions, findings, evidence, drafts, approvals, posted actions, scope, and index state
Write-ownership, row-version or conflict rules, and append-only event-history expectations are defined before concurrent surfaces are implemented
Storage, migration, artifact, and rebuildable search-sidecar boundaries are explicit enough that later work cannot smuggle mutable review state into derived indices or extension-local caches

title: Harness session orchestration
description: Define and implement Rogers supported-harness linkage so Roger can add structure without breaking plain OpenCode fallback, while keeping Gemini support bounded and truthful and making continuity claims auditable.
acceptance_criteria: Harness capability tiers, session ledger, and fallback or return contracts are explicit; OpenCode primary support is real enough for smoke coverage and Gemini bounded support stays truthful; Roger metadata references underlying provider sessions rather than hiding them

title: Implement OpenCode primary adapter, bare-harness dropout, and rr return
description: Implement the real OpenCode path Roger is planning around: provider session start/reopen, ResumeBundle reseed fallback, intentional dropout to plain OpenCode, and explicit return back into Roger. This bead exists because defining the contract alone is not enough; CLI, TUI, and browser-launch claims are not truthful until the primary adapter and fallback loop are operational.
acceptance_criteria:
OpenCode-backed reviews can start or reopen through a SessionLocator when available and fall back to ResumeBundle reseed when direct reopen is unavailable
Intentional dropout preserves the Roger control bundle needed to continue safely in plain OpenCode without losing review target or safety posture
rr return or equivalent helper rebinds the bare harness session to the correct Roger session, with auto-return on harness exit treated only as a convenience path
Smoke coverage proves plain OpenCode fallback and resume behavior are operational rather than marketing language

title: Implement Gemini bounded adapter and truthful reseed path
description: Implement the bounded Gemini path Roger claims for 0.1.0 without implying OpenCode-level continuity. This bead exists to make Tier A support concrete: Roger owns the continuity ledger, captures raw and structured output, and tells the truth when deeper reopen or in-harness affordances do not exist.
acceptance_criteria:
Gemini-backed reviews can start through Roger and persist both raw output artifacts and structured normalization inputs
Capability reporting stays bounded to the Tier A contract and rejects direct-resume, dropout, or harness-native command claims when unsupported
ResumeBundle reseed works truthfully when native reopen semantics are unavailable or weaker than OpenCode
Acceptance coverage proves the bounded Gemini path is useful without parity theater

title: Implement session persistence and resume ledger
description: Implement the Roger-owned session and run ledger that persists enough state to reopen, reseed, or fail closed without relying on ephemeral TUI or harness process memory.
acceptance_criteria: Roger persists canonical session, run, target, and continuity state needed for resume; resume attempts SessionLocator reopen first and uses ResumeBundle reseed as explicit fallback; CLI, TUI, and bridge launches read the same durable session-binding state

title: Prompt pipeline and review engine
description: Build the staged review workflow that explores first, goes deep second, and persists validated structured findings rather than terminal-only output. This epic must encode prompt invocation snapshots, findings-pack validation, repair boundaries, and refresh-safe lineage so downstream TUI and approval flows never act on ad hoc parser output or mutation-capable shortcuts.
acceptance_criteria:
Prompt stages, invocation snapshots, and structured findings boundaries are explicit and durable
Findings normalization and repair behavior are validated so malformed provider output degrades honestly instead of fabricating state
Refresh flows can carry forward, repair, or retire findings coherently without bypassing validation or mutating outbound state implicitly

title: Implement structured findings pack validator and repair boundary
description: Define and implement the StructuredFindingsPack v1 boundary Roger relies on for durable findings, refresh identity, and safe outbound drafting. This bead exists because prompt stages are not enough; Roger needs a validator, salvage rules, and bounded repair behavior so malformed provider output cannot silently manufacture findings or drafts.
acceptance_criteria:
A stable StructuredFindingsPack v1 schema, validator, and normalization boundary into Roger-owned finding rows are explicit
Malformed or partial findings packs follow a bounded repair taxonomy with salvage rules and retry budget rather than ad hoc parser recovery
Raw-only degraded results are preserved and surfaced locally without synthesizing fake findings, fake evidence, or outbound drafts
Integration coverage proves refresh and draft-generation paths consume only validated findings state

title: Session-aware CLI
description: Provide the shell entrypoint for starting, resuming, inspecting, refreshing, and re-entering Roger review sessions using shared launch-routing and session-resolution rules.
acceptance_criteria: CLI can drive launch, resume, findings, status, and refresh without the extension; launch profiles and session re-entry rules are shared with the browser bridge; ambiguity fails closed rather than guessing between sessions or targets

title: Define local launch profiles and daemonless launch-routing rules
description: Define named local launch profiles, terminal or muxer selection, and shared daemonless routing rules so CLI and extension launches resolve the same way without hidden ambient state.
acceptance_criteria: Named launch-profile fields and supported local environments are explicit; CLI and browser-initiated launches share the same routing contract; fallback behavior is truthful when the requested launch environment is unavailable

title: Add session resolution, global session finder, and re-entry routing
description: Implement the rules for resolving the right Roger session from the current repo, handling ambiguity safely, and exposing a global session-finder path for cross-repo re-entry.
acceptance_criteria: A single strong repo-local match can be resumed safely; ambiguous or missing matches fail closed into a session picker instead of guessing; global session finding and re-entry routing work across repos and attention states

title: TUI workflow
description: Implement the main Roger TUI for session navigation, findings triage, linked evidence review, clarification, and local approval of outbound GitHub drafts.
acceptance_criteria: Active review sessions are navigable in the TUI; findings can be triaged and clarified locally without implicit mutation; outbound drafts are reviewed in a distinct local approval queue before posting

title: Add non-mutating clarification in finding detail
description: Add bounded clarification from the finding inspector so users can interrogate a finding locally without implicitly triaging it, drafting it, or mutating GitHub-facing state.
acceptance_criteria: Clarification requests are linked to the finding and session locally; clarification does not mutate triage, outbound, or draft state implicitly; the TUI makes clarification visually distinct from approval or posting actions

title: GitHub integration and extension
description: Add GitHub-targeted adapters and the browser entry surface without letting the extension become the system control plane, state owner, or a backdoor mutation path.
acceptance_criteria: GitHub read and target-resolution behavior is explicit; daemonless launch bridge and install prerequisites are validated; extension UI stays thin, local-first, and non-authoritative

title: Freeze extension packaging, contract export, and release ownership
description: Define the minimal build, contract-export, install, and release shape the extension and Native Messaging bridge actually require. This bead exists because rr-021 is still hiding packaging and installation complexity that can block truthful daemonless bridge claims even when the bridge protocol itself is already decided.
acceptance_criteria:
Minimal TS build tooling is selected with near-zero browser-runtime dependencies and bundler use only if explicitly justified
Rust-owned bridge contract export into the extension tree is defined, including drift detection in local hooks or CI
Roger-owned pack/install and release ownership for companion binaries, host manifests, and browser artifacts is explicit for supported 0.1.0 targets

title: Approval and posting flow
description: Model and implement the local draft, approval, invalidation, and posting path for comments, questions, and suggestions before any GitHub mutation occurs.
acceptance_criteria: Drafts and batches are explicit local objects with approval invalidation rules; posting is an explicit auditable step bound to exact payload and target; no GitHub mutation happens outside Roger approval flow

title: Implement explicit posting flow
description: Implement the only Roger-approved GitHub mutation path: post stored local drafts or batches after explicit approval, target revalidation, and audit capture.
acceptance_criteria: Posting executes only from stored approved draft or batch records; approval binds to exact payload and target identity and is revalidated at post time; partial failure or stale-target conditions fail closed with durable retry and audit state

title: Worktrees and named instances
description: Support isolated review work via worktrees and conservative named-instance sharing without overengineering the first version or guessing between multiple same-PR targets.
acceptance_criteria: Review environments can be isolated safely when needed; multiple named instances can coexist with explicit routing rules; same-PR selection is explicit rather than ambient or guessed

title: Handle same-PR instance selection and launch routing
description: Handle multiple Roger instances or worktrees for the same PR as an explicit routing problem rather than hidden auto-selection.
acceptance_criteria: CLI and extension launches can disambiguate multiple same-PR targets safely; launch-profile compatibility and stale-target recovery are explicit; Roger avoids ambient guessing when more than one plausible local instance exists

title: Hybrid search, memory, and skills
description: Add the repo-first retrieval layer for prior findings, searchable artifacts, and semantic review memory using hybrid lexical plus semantic search from day one, while keeping scope and promotion rules explicit.
acceptance_criteria: Hybrid lexical plus semantic retrieval exists for 0.1.0 with truthful lexical-only degraded mode; Prior review lookup helps refresh flows and memory stays scope-safe; Later memory and promotion workflows remain isolated behind stable interfaces

title: Safety and validation
description: Prove the product stays review-safe, approval-gated, daemonless, and resumable through explicit suites rather than assumptions, with a small but real support matrix across providers, browsers, and platforms.
acceptance_criteria: Validation work is split into traceable suites rather than one catch-all test bead; OpenCode fallback or resume, degraded findings, draft invalidation, and same-PR routing are covered explicitly; supported provider and browser claims stay tied to named suites and fixtures

title: Implement provider acceptance suites for OpenCode and Gemini
description: Turn the claimed OpenCode and Gemini support levels into repeatable acceptance suites so provider support is proven rather than asserted.
acceptance_criteria: OpenCode acceptance covers locator reopen, ResumeBundle fallback, and dropout or return behavior; Gemini acceptance covers Roger-owned continuity, raw plus structured capture, and truthful reseed; unsupported deeper capabilities fail clearly rather than pretending provider parity

title: Validate refresh identity behavior
description: Validate that reruns classify old and new findings correctly instead of duplicating, orphaning, or silently mutating them.
acceptance_criteria: Test scenarios cover carried-forward, resolved, superseded, and stale findings; refresh preserves approval or invalidation lineage correctly; refresh validation uses repeatable fixture-backed scenarios instead of ad hoc manual checks

title: Validate structured findings degraded modes
description: Validate malformed, partial, raw-only, and repair-needed findings outcomes so Roger stays truthful across CLI, TUI, and extension surfaces.
acceptance_criteria: Test scenarios cover valid, partial, raw-only, repair-needed, and retry-exhausted findings outcomes; valid data is salvaged without losing the raw artifact when normalization fails; TUI and extension state stays consistent with degraded findings outcomes

title: Validate draft invalidation, no-status bridge mode, and partial post recovery
description: Validate the safety cases where refresh invalidates drafts, the extension can launch but not read back status, or posting only partially succeeds.
acceptance_criteria: Draft invalidation after refresh, rebase, or target drift is exercised explicitly; launch-only or no-status bridge mode remains honest and does not imply hidden background coordination; partial post success preserves durable retry, audit, and user-attention state without accidental reposting

title: Validate clarification-in-place and OpenCode dropout or return
description: Validate that users can question a finding locally, drop out to plain OpenCode intentionally, and get back to the same Roger session without losing control context.
acceptance_criteria: Clarification from finding detail is non-mutating and locally persisted; OpenCode dropout loads the defined Roger control bundle rather than silently dropping Roger state; return to the same Roger session works or fails closed with explicit recovery guidance

title: Validate session re-entry and same-PR launch routing
description: Validate repo-local re-entry, global session finding, and same-PR launch routing so Roger does not guess between multiple plausible local targets.
acceptance_criteria: Repo-local strong-match resume and ambiguous-match fallback are both exercised; global session finding can route back into the correct session across repos; same-PR multi-instance launches are disambiguated explicitly across CLI and extension surfaces

title: Run architecture risk spikes and capture ADRs
description: Reduce the highest-risk unknowns before implementation spreads: the supported-harness contract, browser bridge, local runtime/process boundary, and artifact/storage split. Record any package-shaping outcomes in ADRs.
acceptance_criteria: Spike outcomes are documented; Any package-shaping decisions are captured in ADRs; The harness capability tiers, browser bridge, and artifact/storage assumptions are no longer implicit
close_reason: Closed by Round 04 reconciliation outcome: architecture spikes were run, accepted ADRs exist, and the formerly implicit assumptions are now explicit in canonical docs.

title: Define core domain schema and finding fingerprint model
description: Specify the first-class entities for sessions, runs, findings, finding fingerprints, code-evidence locations, artifacts, outbound drafts and batches, prompt invocations, SessionLocator and ResumeBundle records, posted actions, scope or memory records, and config layers. Include refresh identity, write-ownership, event-history, approval-invalidation inputs, and scope-boundary rules needed to avoid duplicate explosions, silent bleed, or unsafe draft reuse.
acceptance_criteria:
Core entities and invariants are defined, including prompt invocations, code-evidence locations, draft batches, SessionLocator, and ResumeBundle records
Triage state, outbound state, anchor-validity state, and append-only event history are explicit
Finding fingerprint, refresh classification, approval-invalidation inputs, and scope-boundary rules are explicit

title: Implement local storage, migrations, and artifact budget classes
description: Create the canonical SQLite-family persistence layer, migration flow, row-version or conflict scheme, and artifact split Roger needs for local-first review state. This task must preserve hot-path responsiveness for CLI, TUI, and bridge flows while keeping large artifacts and rebuildable search assets out of mutable core review tables, and it must prove restart or cross-surface access does not quietly reintroduce daemon-style coordination assumptions.
acceptance_criteria:
Migration flow exists for the canonical Roger aggregates and includes explicit row-version or conflict behavior for same-session writes
Storage schema supports sessions, runs, findings, drafts, approvals, posted actions, launch profiles, and index bookkeeping without hiding mutable state in sidecars
Artifact budget classes and cold-storage rules prevent large payloads from bloating primary tables while preserving provenance and rebuild semantics for search sidecars
Storage smoke covers restart, CLI or TUI or bridge re-entry, and recovery of resume plus approval-sensitive state from disk without a resident broker

title: Define the Roger-to-harness session linkage
description: Define the exact Roger session contract for supported harnesses, with OpenCode primary and Gemini bounded: capability tiers, continuity-quality rules, SessionLocator versus ResumeBundle behavior, dropout semantics, and safe optional harness-native commands. This task fixes the contract that downstream adapters and UI surfaces must implement, so Roger can preserve real fallback and truthful provider claims without depending on fragile provider internals.
acceptance_criteria:
Session linkage, capability tiers, continuity-quality reporting, SessionLocator semantics, and ResumeBundle rules are documented and stable enough for adapter implementation
OpenCode Tier B and Gemini Tier A expectations are explicit, including what Roger may not claim for each provider and when direct resume must fall back to reseed
Dropout, return, and safe harness-native command rules are fixed without allowing approval or GitHub-posting behavior to hide inside in-harness commands
The contract names the mandatory smoke scenarios for downstream implementation: locator reopen, stale-locator reseed, plain OpenCode dropout, rr return, and bounded Gemini reseed

title: Encode staged review prompts and structured findings
description: Implement the staged review engine and persist raw outputs plus validated structured findings with evidence, lineage, stage provenance, and honest degraded states.
acceptance_criteria: Prompt stages run independently and persist raw outputs; structured findings are normalized only through the validated StructuredFindingsPack boundary; evidence links, stage provenance, and degraded states are retained honestly

title: Implement refresh behavior and finding lifecycle transitions
description: Support reruns after new commits by classifying findings as carried forward, superseded, resolved, stale, or anchor-invalid instead of duplicating them blindly. This task must emit the refresh signals the approval layer needs so stale context cannot keep an old draft implicitly valid.
acceptance_criteria:
Refresh maps new validated findings to prior findings using fingerprint and anchor rules rather than duplicating them blindly
Lifecycle transitions cover carried-forward, superseded, resolved, stale, and invalid-anchor states
Refresh emits the invalidation or reconfirmation signals outbound drafts need so stale approvals do not survive silent target drift

title: Implement session-aware CLI review, resume, findings, status, and refresh
description: Create the CLI layer that can infer repo context where possible and drive the usable Roger review loop without relying on the extension. This task must make the shell path real for the primary OpenCode workflow, including review, resume, return, findings, status, and refresh, while staying honest about provider support, ambiguous session resolution, and the approval model.
acceptance_criteria:
Review, resume, return, findings, status, and refresh commands work from the shell on the blessed OpenCode path without the extension installed
Repo-context inference resolves PR targets or sessions only when safe, falls back to an explicit picker when ambiguous, and never silently reuses or retargets stale drafts, approvals, or sessions
CLI output surfaces degraded or unsupported provider capabilities truthfully and keeps posting or other mutation-capable actions behind Roger's explicit draft and approval flow
Smoke coverage includes repo-local re-entry, stale-locator fallback to ResumeBundle or clear guidance, and truthful return after bare-harness dropout

title: Build the TUI findings workflow and local approval surfaces
description: Implement the main Roger TUI shell, findings list and detail flow, evidence navigation, clarification hooks, and the local-only approval surface for outbound GitHub drafts.
acceptance_criteria:
Users can enter an active review session, inspect findings and evidence, and understand refresh lineage or degraded state directly in the TUI
Findings can be triaged quickly without triggering GitHub mutation or collapsing approval into ordinary navigation gestures, and bounded clarification remains non-mutating unless the user explicitly requests follow-up work
Outbound drafts can be reviewed, edited, approved, rejected, or invalidated locally before any posting path is exposed, with post-failure recovery still visible in the same queue
notes: Policy: this bead may prepare or open local approval surfaces only; GitHub mutation remains out of scope until rr-008.1 and must stay visibly elevated.

title: Model outbound drafts and posted-action audit trail
description: Define the local representation for review comments, questions, and suggestions awaiting approval, including grouped draft batches, approval invalidation rules, and immutable posted-action lineage.
acceptance_criteria:
Outbound drafts and draft batches are linked to findings, one Roger session, and one immutable review target tuple
Approval binds to exact payload hash and target identity, and affected approvals are invalidated on refresh, rebase, retarget, or worktree or instance changes
Posted payload, posting outcome, and remote identifiers are snapshotted locally so Roger can audit exactly what was approved and what was sent
No posting-capable surface may treat a persisted approval as valid unless refresh and target-identity checks still pass at post time

title: Implement read-safe GitHub adapter and validate the browser launch bridge
description: Resolve PR metadata, anchor targets, and daemonless browser-to-local launch or resume contracts without enabling direct GitHub mutation before the explicit posting flow exists.
acceptance_criteria:
PR targets can be resolved and adapted locally through a Roger-owned adapter that consumes approved draft objects rather than ambient current-PR state or raw gh review-comment commands
Native Messaging primary bridge works on supported browsers including Edge without a persistent daemon, custom URL remains a thin convenience or recovery path only, and no local HTTP or WebSocket bridge becomes required
Bridge and adapter degradation paths are truthful, preserve approval safety, and fail closed when approval, target identity, or local Roger availability is missing
Packaging and install responsibilities for the companion binary and host manifest remain explicit and separate from core Roger CLI or TUI installation
notes: Policy: no raw gh review writes or hidden posting behavior in this bead; bridge work must stay honest about launch-only or no-status modes and install prerequisites.

title: Implement extension UI with bounded v1 status behavior
description: Inject Roger actions into GitHub PR pages, but keep the 0.1.0 extension honest: launch, resume, refresh, and bounded status matter most, and the extension never becomes a state owner or approval surface.
acceptance_criteria: Launch, resume, refresh, and bounded status or no-status behavior are explicit; the extension remains a thin surface over Roger-owned contracts and never owns review state; no posting, approval, or hidden background coordination is introduced here

title: Implement worktree preparation and conservative named-instance sharing
description: Support isolated review environments and multiple named Roger instances with conservative sharing semantics, explicit same-PR routing, and no DB-copy synchronization magic.
acceptance_criteria:
Review worktrees can be prepared, tracked, and torn down with explicit env-copy and mutable-resource rules
Multiple named instances can coexist safely with conservative sharing semantics for DB, ports, and container or service naming
Same-PR ambiguity, missing requested target, and launch-profile fallback paths are explicit rather than guessed
Instance or worktree retargeting behavior is explicit and invalidates affected approvals or review bindings when target identity changes

title: Add hybrid prior-review lookup and memory hooks
description: Provide fast repo-first retrieval of prior findings and review artifacts using lexical plus semantic search, with truthful lexical-only degraded mode and scope-safe memory hooks for refresh and triage.
acceptance_criteria: Prior review lookup is fast and locally queryable with repo-first scope rules; lexical-only degraded mode is explicit when semantic indexing is unavailable; promoted memory remains distinct from broad searchable evidence

title: Map validation matrix, fixtures, and support coverage
description: Run the validation matrix across CLI launch, TUI triage, GitHub launch, approval-gated posting, supported-harness fallback, refresh identity, and provider acceptance so the planning stage exits with proof rather than optimism. This task should be the place where Roger demonstrates that local-first, daemonless, approval-safe behavior actually survives the real seams between storage, session orchestration, TUI, extension, and instance-routing work.
acceptance_criteria:
Validation artifacts map the major flow families plus blessed provider, browser, OS, and fixture paths to repeatable smoke or integration coverage
CLI, TUI, and GitHub launch paths have repeatable validation steps covering the blessed OpenCode path, bounded Gemini path, and browser bridge truthfulness
Nothing posts without explicit approval: cross-review target mismatch, stale approval after refresh, missing-local-state cases, and partial post recovery all fail closed or recover honestly
OpenCode locator resume, stale-locator ResumeBundle reseed, bare-harness dropout, rr return, refresh identity carry-forward, same-PR instance routing, and daemonless bridge behavior are exercised explicitly rather than inferred from unit tests alone
